title: Queen-and-Knight Net
setup: Your knight on **f5** and king on **f6** lock the f-file — the queen just needs the right entry.
hint: Bring the queen to the 8th rank along the diagonal — the knight already shadows {{e7}}.
explanation:
## The Idea

A delightful **queen-and-knight cooperation**. The knight on f5 quietly covers {{e7}} and the queen swoops in along the long h1-a8 diagonal... but in fact along the rank from h1 to h8.

Play {{Qh8#}}.

Why is the king stuck?

- {{e8}} — covered by the knight on **f5** (knight jump f5-e7? no, the queen on h8 attacks the 8th rank)
- {{g8}} and {{e8}} — covered by the queen along the 8th rank
- {{e7}} — controlled by the knight on **f5**
- {{f7}} — blocked by Black's own pawn
- {{g7}} — covered by the queen on h8 (diagonal)
- Capturing? Kxh8 is impossible — the king is on f8, not adjacent

> **Takeaway:** Knights and queens combine into mating nets because the knight covers squares the queen can't (close diagonal/horse-jumps). Always scan for these dual coverage patterns.
